tools.name: applyClangFormat
tools.description: Format C/C++ source files in-place using clang-format. Provide ABSOLUTE file paths or ABSOLUTE glob patterns (e.g. 'D:/proj/src/**/*.cpp'). Directory paths without a glob are rejected. Non-C/C++ files are silently skipped. Style resolution: explicit `style` arg > CLANG_FORMAT_CONFIG (file) > CLANG_FORMAT_STYLE > clang-format auto-detection.

files.description: Absolute file paths or absolute glob patterns. Relative paths are resolved against cwd with a warning.

style.description: Optional explicit style (LLVM, Google, file, file:<path>, ...). Overrides environment defaults.

findings.title: High entropy description in 'applyClangFormat'
findings.description: Unusual character distribution may indicate obfuscated content.
findings.remediation: Review description for base64-encoded or otherwise obfuscated payloads.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: initialize accepts type-confused protocolVersion (integer instead of string)
findings.description: The server accepted an initialize request where protocolVersion was an integer (42) instead of a string. Strict type checking prevents parser confusion and unexpected coercion behavior.
findings.remediation: Validate that protocolVersion is a string before processing.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 1 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.

findings.title: Server accepts path traversal cursor without validation
findings.description: tools/list accepted cursor='../../../../etc/passwd' and returned a result instead of rejecting it. This may indicate missing cursor validation.
findings.remediation: Validate cursor values; reject non-opaque or path-like cursor strings.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
